Custom CAN Signals (Advanced)

Press any key or click outside to close

Load Layout JSON

Paste a layout in firmware JSON format (the same shape an export produces, or what an AI builder generates). It loads straight into the editor for preview — no device needed. Hit Save afterwards to push it to a connected dash.

Select ECU / Vehicle

Dashboard Switcher

Pick which layouts the on-dash left/right arrows cycle through, and the order. Leave nothing checked to cycle every layout in filesystem order.

Loading...

Storage Manager

Files must be on internal storage to be used by the dashboard. Use SD card for backup and transfer.

Vehicle & channels
Channels
The vocabulary your dash speaks — RPM, Coolant, Lambda. Tune thresholds and bind to signals.
—
Live Signals
Raw CAN signals as they arrive. Live values, min/max, staleness.
—
Import DBC
Load a DBC file from your tuning software — bulk-import signal definitions.
CSV / DBC supported
Custom CAN Signals
Hand-decode one-off CAN frames not covered by a preset — set CAN ID, bit range, scale and offset.
Advanced
Create Preset
Author a reusable ECU preset from scratch — saved to the device and shown in the source picker.
/lfs/presets
Connectivity
WiFi & network
Current SSID, IP, hotspot status. Configure WiFi on the dash's touchscreen Settings.
—
OBD2
Mode 01 + Mode 22 polling. Trouble codes, vehicle info, custom PIDs.
—
Device
Device Info
Firmware version, device id, free heap, OTA update.
—
Dimmer & Night
Brightness curve, night-mode trigger, auto-dim from ignition.
Configurable
Gear Calc
Calculate gear from RPM + speed. Wheel + final drive + ratios.
CALCULATED_GEAR
Fuel Sender
Calibrate fuel-level sender curve. Multi-point linear.
Calibrate

Record CAN signals to SD card. Selectable rate, replay, and share raw traces.

Data Logger
Signal Log — decoded values at a fixed rate. Good for plotting + replaying through dashboard widgets.
Raw CAN — every frame, exact timing, SavvyCAN-compatible CSV. Good for offline DBC decoding.
Idle
Replaying...
Logs
Share Raw CAN Trace
Upload a Raw CAN recording tagged with your car so it can be debugged off-device. Stays private to the project maintainers.
Fuel Calibration
With a known fuel level in the tank, tap the matching button to set the calibration point. Do Empty first, then Full.
Loading...
Gear Setup — Calculated Gear
Back-computes the engaged gear from live RPM & vehicle speed. Enter your gearbox ratios (in gear order), final drive, and wheel circumference. The signal CALCULATED_GEAR publishes 0 for Neutral / stationary, 1 for 1st, etc.
Gearbox Ratios (N = 0.000; 1st–Nth in order)
Sync tip: drive in that gear above 5 km/h, then tap Sync. The ratio is back-calculated from live RPM and speed using your wheel circumference and final drive.
ODOMETER live: —
Auto-accumulates from your selected Speed Signal. Persists every 1 km or 5 min. Enter a starting reading once to match the vehicle's factory odo.
OBD2 Setup
CAN bus … ECU preset … Enabled signals … Custom PIDs …
OBD2 Protocols responding
Loading…
1. Set your ECU preset (Setup › ECU Preset) so the dash knows which OBD2 modes your vehicle speaks (Mode 22 / KWP / standard differ).
2. Map OBD2 signals to channels in the Channels editor — that's the single place every signal (CAN, OBD2, internal) binds now.
3. Add Custom PIDs for forum-documented values your ECU doesn't expose as standard.
4. Read Trouble Codes when the check-engine light is on.
Quick ECU Setup One-click: applies a whole ECU's CAN map to all channels. Fine-tune any channel later in the Channels editor.
Show every preset. Detected ones are marked.
Detected presets have a blue dot — their broadcast CAN IDs overlap with what the dash saw during the last Scan Car. Run a fresh scan from Device Settings if you've swapped vehicles.
Trouble Codes
Code Description
Codes come from Mode 03/07/0A. Clear fires Mode 04.
Vehicle Info
VIN … ECU name … Last read never
OBD2 Custom PIDs
Define OBD2 PIDs from forum threads or vehicle service manuals. Picking + Add opens a form with a Test button that queries the ECU once and shows the raw bytes + decoded value, so you can verify the decode formula before saving.
Signal Mode PID Decode Live
Saved with your layout. Use Export to share with others. Imported packs merge into the current list (duplicates by signal name overwrite). Hit Save Layout in the header to persist changes to the device.
Custom PID
Decode
Formula: value = raw × scale + offset
Polling
Test
Hit Test to query the ECU.
Fuel Sender Setup — Calibration Curve
Map the fuel sender's analog voltage to a tank reading. Add a point for each known reference level (most senders are non-linear, so 3–5 points improve accuracy). For each point, type the tank reading (% or litres) and capture the live voltage with Use current. Points are sorted automatically.
Live voltage -- V
Calibration points 0 / 8
Voltage Tank reading
Brightness Dimmer
Developer Options
Bench / debugging aids. None of these change your layout or the device's behaviour in the car.
Fakes a virtual ECU so OBD2 gauges (e.g. fuel level) move on the bench with no car connected. Synthesizes Mode 01 polling, freeze frame, DTC read/clear, and VIN/ECU-name responses. Not saved; off after a reboot.
Draw gauges/text in the editor canvas when no live device frame is present. The device's LVGL render is always the source of truth. Editor-only.
Simulate the real dash in the offline preview: RGB565 colour banding + firmware-feasible glow (no Gaussian blur / radial halos). Editor preview only.
W 0
